_readme: Populated automatically by scripts/scout_watcher.py (runs locally on the Mac, triggered by the dashboard's Run button) as you drop TikTok links and click Run — real stats/caption/audio pulled straight from TikTok's own page data, audio transcribed locally with Whisper. Now a rolling log (capped at the newest 20 entries), not a one-time snapshot. The strategist_agent.sh cron job on the droplet reads this whenever 'collected_at' is newer than its last-processed date and turns new entries into video-scripts-queue.json script ideas. Entries can also still be added by hand from a live browsing session if useful.

entries.format: cinematic aesthetic edit — moody film-style clips (car/city at night), bold white centered text overlay, short loop
entries.audio_note: moody instrumental/voice clip, matches the cinematic tone
entries.why_it_works: extremely high save-to-like ratio — aspirational/quotable content people bookmark to rewatch or resend, not just react to

entries.format: carousel listicle — one job title + pay range per slide, movie stills as the visual instead of stock photos or plain text
entries.hook_text: Finance Jobs That Can Make You a Millionaire
entries.why_it_works: real numbers + recognizable movie imagery per slide gives concrete, save-worthy value instead of generic advice

entries.format: single real example as proof — an actual detailed resume shown in full, text overlay pointing to creator's page for help
entries.hook_text: Make use of this time off by updating your resume!
entries.why_it_works: exceptional save rate — a real, specific, detailed artifact people want to reference later beats generic advice every time

entries.hook_text: #finance #financetok #fyp #wallstreet #nyc  Many of the highest paying careers in finance involve analyzing companies, managing large amounts of capital, and making investment decisions that can impact billions of dollars. Roles like investment banking, private equity, hedge funds, quantitative trading, and portfolio management are known for extremely high compensation, but they are also some of the most competitive fields in business. These careers usually require strong analytical skills, understanding financial markets, knowledge of valuation and investing, and often degrees in finance, economics, mathematics, or business. Many professionals start in roles like analysts and gradually move up as they gain experience and build a track record of performance. While the earning potential in these careers can be enormous, success typically requires long hours, discipline, and deep knowledge of markets and investments. Learning about these roles early can help students understand the different paths available in finance and what skills they might want to develop if they are interested in careers involving investing, corporate finance, or financial markets. Which one of these careers interests you the most?
